name: Billing complaint — resolved
transcript:
Customer: I was charged $49.99 twice for my subscription. This is unacceptable.
Agent: I apologize for the inconvenience. Let me look into that right away. I can see the duplicate charge. I've processed a refund of $49.99 to your card. You should see it within 3-5 business days.
Customer: Thank you, that's great.
Agent: Is there anything else I can help you with today?
Customer: No, that's all. Thanks for fixing it so quickly.

name: Product defect — escalated
transcript:
Customer: Your product is broken. It stopped working after one day. I want a refund.
Agent: I'm sorry to hear that. Can you tell me more about what happened?
Customer: It just won't turn on. I followed all the instructions.
Agent: I understand your frustration. This seems like a hardware defect. Let me transfer you to our specialist team who can process a replacement or full refund.
Customer: Fine, but this is really frustrating.
Agent: I completely understand. I'm connecting you now.

key_facts: product stopped working after one day

name: Shipping inquiry — resolved
transcript:
Customer: Hi, I placed an order three days ago and haven't received a shipping confirmation yet.
Agent: Let me check that for you. Can I have your order number?
Customer: It's order 12345.
Agent: I see your order. It was processed yesterday and should ship today. You'll get a tracking email by end of day.
Customer: Oh great, so it's on its way?
Agent: Yes, it's being packed now. Expected delivery is Friday.
Customer: Perfect, thanks!

key_facts: tracking email by end of day

name: Subscription cancellation — unresolved
transcript:
Customer: I want to cancel my subscription.
Agent: I'm sorry to hear that. May I ask why?
Customer: I just don't use it anymore.
Agent: I understand. I can process the cancellation, but your access will end at the current billing cycle. Are you sure?
Customer: Yes, cancel it.
Agent: Okay, I've submitted the cancellation request. You'll receive a confirmation email.
Customer: When does it actually end?
Agent: At the end of your current billing period, which is March 15th.
Customer: That's still two weeks away. Can you end it now?
Agent: I'm sorry, our policy doesn't allow mid-cycle cancellations. I can escalate this to a supervisor if you'd like.
Customer: Forget it, just cancel it.

name: Technical support — resolved
transcript:
Customer: I can't log into my account. It says invalid password.
Agent: Have you tried resetting your password?
Customer: Yes, I didn't get the email.
Agent: Let me check. Can you confirm the email on your account?
Customer: john@example.com
Agent: I see the account. The reset email was sent but might be in spam. Let me send a new one. Done. Check your inbox now.
Customer: Got it! I can log in now. Thanks.
Agent: Great! Make sure to check your spam folder if you don't see emails from us.
Customer: Will do, thanks for the help.

expected.intent: login issue — password reset email not received

name: Hallucination test — agent makes false claim
transcript:
Customer: What's the status of my order?
Agent: Your order shipped yesterday and will arrive tomorrow.
Customer: Really? I never got a confirmation email.
Agent: The system shows it was confirmed on January 5th.
Customer: I placed the order on January 10th.
Agent: Let me check again. You're right, I apologize. The order was placed January 10th and is still being processed. It should ship within 48 hours.
Customer: Okay, that's more like it.

expected.intent: order status inquiry with incorrect initial information

name: Angry customer — negative sentiment
transcript:
Customer: This is absolutely terrible! I've been waiting for THREE HOURS for support! Nobody cares about your customers!
Agent: I sincerely apologize for the wait. I understand how frustrating this is. How can I help you?
Customer: My internet has been down since morning! I work from home and I'm losing money!
Agent: That's unacceptable. Let me run a diagnostic on your line right now. I can see there's an outage in your area. Technicians are on site and it should be restored within the hour.
Customer: An hour?! I've already lost half a day!
Agent: I understand. I'll credit your account for the full day. And I'll call you personally when it's restored.
Customer: Fine. But this better not happen again.

expected.intent: internet outage complaint with service credit

name: Positive experience
transcript:
Customer: I just wanted to call and say your service is amazing! The new feature is exactly what I needed.
Agent: Thank you so much! That really means a lot. We worked hard on that update.
Customer: The setup was so easy. I had it running in five minutes.
Agent: That's great to hear. We designed it to be simple. Is there anything else you'd like to see?
Customer: Maybe a dark mode? But honestly, I'm happy with everything.
Agent: I'll pass that suggestion along. Thanks for taking the time to call!

name: Confused customer — mixed outcome
transcript:
Customer: I'm not sure I understand my bill. There are charges I don't recognize.
Agent: Let me walk you through it. The first charge is your monthly subscription of $29.99.
Customer: Okay, that I understand.
Agent: The second charge is $9.99 for the premium add-on you signed up for last month.
Customer: I don't remember signing up for that.
Agent: It was activated on December 1st. Would you like me to disable it?
Customer: Yes, disable it. But I still don't think I should be charged.
Agent: I can issue a one-time credit for this month. Going forward you won't be charged.
Customer: I guess that's fair. But I wish these things were clearer.
Agent: I completely understand. I'll pass that feedback along.

name: Short call — minimal data
transcript:
Customer: Hi, I need to update my address.
Agent: Sure. What's your new address?
Customer: 123 Main Street, Apt 4B, New York, NY 10001.
Agent: Done. Your address has been updated.
Customer: Thanks.
